profile.title: My profile
profile.subtitle: Your account, organisation and SafeVoice access. Read-only.
profile.accessNote: Permissions are managed by your administrator in Users & permissions.

footer.rights: © {{year}} DSV Corporation — RegulaOne. All rights reserved.

auth.redirectingHint: You will return to SafeVoice once signed in.
auth.loopBody: We kept getting sent back to the login page. This usually means your session cookie is not valid for this address.
auth.loopHint1: Make sure you are opening SafeVoice on the same host as RegulaOne.
auth.loopHint2: Confirm the RegulaOne backend sets the session cookie for host {{host}}.
auth.loopHint3: Try signing in again from the central RegulaOne app.
auth.errorBody: Please try again in a moment.
auth.signInRequiredBody: You must sign in to open the SafeVoice workspace.

access.disabledBody: Your RegulaOne account has been disabled, so SafeVoice access is paused. Please contact your administrator.
access.moduleTitle: SafeVoice is not in your plan
access.moduleBody: Your organisation's subscription does not include the SafeVoice whistleblower module. Add it in RegulaOne to gain access.
access.packageBody: Your organisation's subscription has expired, so access to SafeVoice is paused. Renew the plan in RegulaOne to restore access.
access.permissionBody: Your account does not have any SafeVoice role yet. Ask your administrator to grant a SafeVoice permission (for example Investigator or Compliance Officer).
access.needHelp: Need help? Contact your RegulaOne administrator.
access.deniedBody: You do not have permission to view this page. If you believe this is a mistake, contact your administrator.
access.notFoundBody: The page you are looking for does not exist or has moved.
access.invalidTenantBody: This report page must be opened through your organisation's own secure reporting link. Without it we cannot tell which company the report is for, so the form is not shown and nothing is submitted.
access.invalidTenantExampleLabel: A valid reporting link looks like
access.invalidTenantHint: Your organisation shares this link on notice boards, intranet pages, or your employment documents. Contact your employer or compliance officer if you cannot find it.

report.title: Submit an anonymous report
report.categoryHint: Choose the area that best matches what you are reporting.
report.hrSeparateBody: Individual labour disputes are handled as HR matters, not anonymous whistleblower cases, and do not receive a tracking PIN.
report.factsPlaceholder: Describe what happened, when, and which process was involved. Avoid details that could identify you unless they are necessary for follow-up.
report.factsHint: Do not include your name or personal details unless strictly necessary.
report.evidenceDrop: Drop PDF, PNG, JPG, XML or DOCX evidence here, or click to choose
report.evidenceTypes: Max 10 MB per file. Filenames and metadata are removed automatically.
report.channelTitle: How would you like to report?
report.channelOralInfo: Call the confidential line on +48 22 000 00 00 (Mon–Fri, 9:00–17:00). You will receive a tracking code by the same secure process.
report.requestMeeting: I would like a face-to-face meeting
report.requestMeetingInfo: Under EU 2019/1937 you may request an in-person meeting within a reasonable timeframe. Leave a way to be contacted that does not reveal your identity (e.g. the secure message thread after submitting).
report.anonymousBody: We never collect your IP address, device or browser details, or location. Your identity is protected by law.
report.consentPrivacy: I have read the {{privacyLink}} and understand how my report and any personal data will be handled.
report.consentVoluntary: I am reporting voluntarily and to the best of my knowledge the information is true.

safeguards.ack: Acknowledgement within 7 days and feedback within 3 months.
safeguards.noTelemetry: No reporter telemetry is shown to case handlers.
safeguards.deletion: Irrelevant personal data is deleted within a short window.
safeguards.external: You can also report to an external authority at any time.

evidence.dropPrompt: Drop files here, or click to choose
evidence.fileTypes: PDF, PNG, JPG, XML or DOCX · max 10 MB each · up to {{max}} files
evidence.unsupported: Unsupported file type. Allowed: PDF, PNG, JPG, XML, DOCX.
evidence.tooLarge: File is too large. Maximum size is 10 MB.
evidence.tooMany: You can attach at most {{max}} files.
evidence.empty: That file is empty, so it was skipped.
evidence.readError: Could not read that file. Please try again.
evidence.noPreview: Preview is not available for this file type. Use Download to open it.

success.title: Your report has been received
success.subtitle: Below is your access key — the ONE credential for your case. It is shown only once and is the only way to follow your report.
success.accessKeyHint: This 64-character key is both your case reference and your password. Keep it secret.
success.saveWarning: Save this key somewhere safe, outside this application. We store only a one-way fingerprint of it — we cannot recover or reset it. If you lose it, the case cannot be reopened, which is what keeps you anonymous.
success.fileHeading: SafeVoice access key — keep this secret and safe
success.hrBody: As an individual labour matter this was sent to HR and is handled outside the anonymous whistleblower channel, so no access key is issued.
success.ackInfo: You will receive an acknowledgement within 7 days and feedback on your report within 3 months, through the tracking channel.

track.title: Track a report
track.subtitle: Anonymous status lookup
track.keyHint: This is the single key shown to you once when you submitted. We never stored it, so it cannot be recovered.
track.notFound: We could not find a report with that code and PIN. Check both and try again.
track.detailsSub: The details you submitted (only you can read this)
track.evidenceSub: Files you attached to this report
track.noEvidence: You did not attach any files to this report.
track.emptyThread: No messages yet. A case handler will be in touch here.
track.messagePlaceholder: Write a message to the case handler…
track.sendError: Your message could not be sent. Please try again.
track.closedGraceInfo: This case has been closed. You can still send one final message until {{time}}.
track.closedEnded: This case is closed and the reply window has ended. You can still read the conversation.

dashboard.title: Case operations
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of whistleblower cases for your organisation.

cases.title: Case register
cases.subtitle: All whistleblower cases for your organisation.
cases.searchPlaceholder: Search by case reference or category

case.evidenceSub: Original file names are hidden to protect privacy.
case.messagePlaceholder: Request a follow-up detail without asking the reporter to identify themselves.
case.composerLocked: Assign an investigator and move the case past “Received” before replying to the reporter.
case.composerClosedLocked: This case is closed — staff can no longer message the reporter. Reopen the case to reply.
case.unreadHint: Unread — click to mark as read
case.statusMessagePlaceholder: Add a short update to send to the reporter with this status change…
case.closeNotice: Closing locks the thread for staff. The reporter can still send a final message for 48 hours; after that the thread closes for everyone.
case.reopenBody: You are reopening a closed case and changing its status to “{{value}}”. Please give a reason — it is recorded in the audit trail.
case.reopenReasonPlaceholder: Explain why this closed case is being reopened…
case.reopenReasonRequired: A reason is required to reopen a closed case.
case.confirmStatusBody: This will update the case status to “{{value}}” and record an immutable audit entry.
case.confirmExportBody: A redacted summary will be generated. Reporter identity is never included. This action is logged.
case.confirmReviewedBody: This records that you have reviewed the case. The action is logged in the audit trail.

inbox.title: Secure inbox
inbox.subtitle: Case threads
inbox.selectThread: Select a case thread to view the conversation.

audit.title: Security audit trail
audit.subtitle: Immutable, tamper-evident activity log.
audit.empty: No audit entries match your search.
audit.confirmExportBody: The current view will be exported as a signed CSV. This export is itself recorded in the audit trail.

users.title: Users and permissions
users.subtitle: Authorised personnel and the role permission matrix.
users.matrixSub: What each role is allowed to do
users.confirmRemoveBody: This officer will lose access to SafeVoice immediately. The change is logged.

security.mfaValue: Required for all staff roles; no bypass role.
security.revocationValue: Immediate revocation on role change or suspected compromise.
security.rateLimitValue: Public tracking and intake endpoints are rate-limited without fingerprinting.
security.telemetryValue: No analytics, marketing pixels, or browser fingerprinting.
security.monitoringValue: Security operations only; not exposed to case investigators.

retention.defaultValue: Three years after the case is closed, in line with the Polish 2024 Act.
retention.irrelevantValue: Personal data not relevant to a report is deleted within 14 days.
retention.holdValue: Preserves case materials when a legal hold applies.
retention.destructionValue: Scheduled, logged deletion once retention ends.

legal.basisValue: Legal obligation, protected follow-up, anti-retaliation handling, and GDPR storage limitation.
legal.respValue: Authorised officers handle cases; security operations handle access monitoring; HR handles separated grievances.

privacy.title: Privacy notice
privacy.intro: This notice explains how DSV Corporation Pty Ltd (“we”), as data controller, processes personal data when you use the SafeVoice whistleblower channel. It is provided under Articles 13–14 of the GDPR.
privacy.controllerBody: Data controller: DSV Corporation Pty Ltd, operating the RegulaOne platform in Poland. Our EU representative and Data Protection Officer can be contacted at dpo@regulaone.eu.
privacy.dataBody: For anonymous reports we deliberately collect as little as possible: the report category, dates, the facts you provide, and any evidence you choose to attach. We do not collect your IP address, device or browser fingerprint, or location. If you choose to identify yourself, we process the identifying data you give us.
privacy.basisBody: We process reports to comply with a legal obligation (the Polish Whistleblower Protection Act of 14 June 2024 and Directive (EU) 2019/1937) under Article 6(1)(c) GDPR, and to protect the public interest.
privacy.retentionBody: Personal data in a report is kept for three years after the case is closed. Personal data that is not relevant to handling the report is deleted within 14 days.
privacy.rightsBody: You have the right of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, and to lodge a complaint with the President of the Personal Data Protection Office (UODO). These rights may be restricted where necessary to protect a whistleblower's identity (Article 23 GDPR).
privacy.recipientsBody: Only officers authorised in writing can access reports, and they are bound by confidentiality. Your identity is never revealed to the persons concerned by a report.
privacy.complaintBody: You can lodge a complaint with the Polish supervisory authority, the President of the Personal Data Protection Office (Prezes Urzędu Ochrony Danych Osobowych, UODO), uodo.gov.pl.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the SafeVoice whistleblower channel. By using the channel you agree to use it in good faith.
terms.useBody: SafeVoice is for reporting breaches in good faith. Knowingly reporting false information may not be protected and can carry legal consequences.
terms.protectionBody: Reporters acting on reasonable grounds are protected from retaliation under the Polish Whistleblower Protection Act and Directive (EU) 2019/1937.
terms.scopeBody: Individual labour disputes and interpersonal conflicts are handled by HR and fall outside the whistleblower channel.

cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.intro: SafeVoice uses the minimum storage required to function. We do not use analytics, advertising, or tracking cookies.
cookies.necessaryBody: A secure session cookie (httpOnly) keeps staff signed in. It cannot be read by scripts and is never used for tracking. A small local entry remembers your language choice.
cookies.consentBody: Because we only use strictly necessary storage, no consent is required under the ePrivacy Directive. We will ask for consent before introducing any non-essential cookies.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.intro: We are committed to making SafeVoice usable by everyone, in line with EN 301 549 and WCAG 2.1 Level AA, as required by the European Accessibility Act (Directive (EU) 2019/882).
accessibility.statusBody: SafeVoice aims to conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA. We test keyboard navigation, screen-reader support, colour contrast, and zoom/reflow.
accessibility.feedbackBody: If you encounter an accessibility barrier, contact accessibility@regulaone.eu and we will respond within a reasonable timeframe.

external.title: External reporting
external.intro: You are always free to report to an external authority instead of, or in addition to, this internal channel.
external.rpoBody: The Polish Commissioner for Human Rights (Rzecznik Praw Obywatelskich) is the central authority for external whistleblower reports. Visit brpo.gov.pl for details.
external.euBody: For breaches of EU law you may also contact the relevant EU institution or body.
external.note: Reporting externally does not remove your legal protection.

howItWorks.title: How your report is handled
howItWorks.intro: This page explains, step by step, what happens after you submit a report.
howItWorks.step1Body: You send your report through this channel, by phone, or by requesting a meeting. You stay anonymous unless you choose otherwise.
howItWorks.step2Body: We confirm we received your report within seven days, via your tracking code.
howItWorks.step3Body: An authorised officer reviews the report impartially and may ask follow-up questions through the secure thread.
howItWorks.step4Body: Within three months we tell you what action has been taken or planned.
howItWorks.step5Body: The case is closed and records are kept only as long as the law requires.

dataRequest.title: Data subject request
dataRequest.subtitle: Exercise your GDPR rights
dataRequest.success: Your request has been recorded. We will respond within one month (Article 12 GDPR).
dataRequest.note: Some rights may be limited where they would reveal a whistleblower's identity.

cookieBanner.message: SafeVoice uses only strictly necessary storage to keep you signed in and remember your language. We use no analytics or tracking.

validation.futureDate: The date cannot be in the future.
validation.email: Please enter a valid email address.
validation.fileType: Unsupported file type. Allowed: PDF, PNG, JPG, XML, DOCX.
validation.fileSize: File is too large. Maximum size is 10 MB.
validation.consentRequired: Please confirm both statements to continue.

toast.downloaded: Key downloaded — store the file somewhere safe.
toast.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

landing.badge: EU Whistleblower Directive & Polish 2024 Act compliant
landing.subtitle: A secure, anonymous channel for reports — and one workspace to handle every case.
landing.description: SafeVoice connects RegulaOne SSO, encrypted anonymous intake, secure two-way messaging, role-based case handling, audit trails, and statutory retention into one compliant whistleblowing platform.
landing.capabilitiesTitle: Everything a compliant whistleblowing channel needs
landing.capabilitiesSubtitle: Built for the EU Whistleblower Directive (2019/1937) and Poland's 2024 Whistleblower Protection Act.
landing.cap1Desc: Reports and attachments are encrypted (AES-256-GCM); reporters stay anonymous with a single secure access key.
landing.cap2Desc: Follow up with the reporter through an encrypted channel without ever revealing their identity.
landing.cap3Desc: Every action is logged in an immutable trail, with statutory retention windows kept ready for inspection.
landing.cap4Desc: SSO sign-in with backend-enforced SafeVoice roles, tenant isolation, and least-privilege navigation.
landing.vaultDesc: Reporter identity and metadata are never exposed to case handlers — anonymity is enforced by design.
